features.name: SRS-Driven Review Mode
features.description: A dedicated /review route that fetches words due for spaced repetition review (GET /srs/due), runs them through a quiz, and submits quality ratings (0–5) to the SM-2 backend (POST /srs/review). This connects the existing but currently disconnected SRS backend to the frontend quiz.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want the app to automatically surface words I need to review today so that I reinforce vocabulary at the optimal moment for long-term retention.
acceptance_criteria: Calling GET /srs/due returns words where nextReview <= now for the authenticated user
After completing a review quiz, each word's SM-2 state (interval, easinessFactor, repetitions, nextReview) is updated via POST /srs/review
Words answered correctly receive quality 4 or 5; words answered incorrectly or timed out receive quality 0–2
The review session ends when the due queue is exhausted
If no words are due, the UI shows a 'Nothing due today' state with the time until next review

features.name: Vocabulary Strength Dashboard
features.description: A dashboard component that displays each word's strength tier derived from existing SRS fields: Weak (repetitions < 2 or interval ≤ 1), Medium (repetitions 2–4 or interval 2–7), Strong (isLearned === true or interval > 7). Shows counts per tier and a visual breakdown.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see how many words I know weakly, moderately, and strongly so that I can gauge my overall progress and identify gaps.
acceptance_criteria: A GET /srs/stats endpoint returns counts of words in each strength tier for the authenticated user
The dashboard displays Weak, Medium, and Strong word counts with a simple visual (e.g., bar or pie)
Strength tiers are computed server-side from UserWordProgress fields without schema changes
Dashboard updates immediately after completing a review session

features.name: Daily Streak
features.description: A streak counter that increments each calendar day the learner completes at least one review session. Resets to 0 on a missed day. Displayed prominently on the home page. A streak freeze mechanic (2 per week, auto-applied) can follow as a V2 addition.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see my consecutive days of study so that I have a concrete, daily reason to open the app.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains fields: currentStreak (number), longestStreak (number), lastActivityDate (Date)
POST /srs/review updates lastActivityDate and increments currentStreak if lastActivityDate was yesterday; resets to 1 if more than 1 day has passed
longestStreak is updated whenever currentStreak exceeds it
The home page displays the current streak count
A missed day reduces currentStreak to 0 (no freeze mechanic in V1)

features.name: Flashcard Exercise Type
features.description: A flip-card exercise format where the learner sees the Uzbek word, then taps to reveal the translation, and self-rates their recall (e.g., 'Got it' / 'Missed it'). Maps to SM-2 quality 5 (got it) or 1 (missed it). Simpler to implement than timed multiple-choice because no answer validation is needed.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a low-pressure flashcard mode so that I can self-assess my recall without the stress of a timed quiz.
acceptance_criteria: A questionType field is added to the quiz state machine (values: 'multiple-choice' | 'flashcard')
Flashcard view shows the Uzbek word on the front and translation on the back after tap
Two action buttons: 'Got it' (submits quality 5) and 'Missed it' (submits quality 1)
Session ends and submits all ratings to POST /srs/review in bulk or per-card
Accessible from the review route as an alternative to multiple-choice

features.name: Lesson and Unit Structure
features.description: Words are grouped into named Lessons (e.g., 'Greetings', 'Numbers 1–20', 'Food and Drink'), and lessons into Topics. A lesson map on the home page lets learners pick a lesson to study. Completing a lesson enrolls its words in the SRS queue for future reviews.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want vocabulary organised into thematic lessons so that I can study related words together and see where I am in the curriculum.
acceptance_criteria: A Lesson schema is created: { id, title, topic, language, wordIds: UUID[], order: number }
GET /lessons returns all lessons for a language, ordered by topic and lesson order
POST /lessons/:id/start enrolls all lesson words in SRS (creates UserWordProgress records with nextReview = now)
The frontend shows a lesson map with status: Not Started / In Progress / Complete
A lesson is Complete when all its words have isLearned === true
Word schema gains optional lessonId and topic fields

features.name: Progress Statistics Page
features.babbel_analog: Stats summary and learning time tracking
features.description: A /stats page showing: total words reviewed, words by strength tier, current and longest streak, total review sessions completed, and lessons started vs. completed. Data is aggregated from existing SRS and streak fields.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a single page that shows my overall learning progress so that I can feel a sense of accomplishment and identify what to focus on next.
acceptance_criteria: GET /users/me/stats returns: totalWordsReviewed, wordsByTier {weak, medium, strong}, currentStreak, longestStreak, totalSessions, lessonsStarted, lessonsCompleted
Stats page renders all of the above fields
Page is accessible from the main navigation
Data reflects the authenticated user's own history only

features.name: Weekly Study Goals
features.babbel_analog: Weekly lesson goals with progress ring
features.description: Learners set a weekly target (e.g., 5 review sessions per week). The home page shows a weekly progress ring (e.g., '3 of 5 this week'). The goal resets each Monday. No notifications in V1.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to set a weekly practice goal so that I have a concrete target to work toward and don't feel like a missed day ruins everything.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains: weeklyGoal (number, default 5), weeklySessionsCompleted (number), weeklyGoalResetDate (Date)
POST /srs/review increments weeklySessionsCompleted; resets it to 0 and updates weeklyGoalResetDate if a new week has started
PATCH /users/me/goal accepts { weeklyGoal: number } and validates 1 ≤ weeklyGoal ≤ 30
Home page shows weekly progress ring
A goal-setting prompt appears during onboarding (F-09) and is editable from settings

features.name: Fill-in-the-Blank Exercise
features.description: A sentence is shown with one word blanked out. The learner types the missing Uzbek word. Requires example sentences to be associated with each word. Answer matching is case-insensitive and trims whitespace. Maps to SM-2 quality 4 on correct, 1 on incorrect.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to type words in context rather than always selecting from a list so that I build active recall and spelling skills.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema gains optional exampleSentence (string) and exampleSentenceTranslation (string) fields
Fill-in-the-blank questions only appear for words that have an exampleSentence
Answer validation is case-insensitive and trims surrounding whitespace
On incorrect answer, the correct word is shown with the full sentence
questionType 'fill-in-the-blank' added to the quiz state machine

features.name: Onboarding Intake Flow
features.description: A 3-screen onboarding flow shown to new users after first login. Collects: reason for learning Uzbek (heritage, travel, work, curiosity), self-assessed level (beginner, some knowledge, conversational), and weekly goal. Stores these on the User record and uses them to recommend a starting lesson.
features.user_story: As a new user, I want to tell the app why I'm learning and how much I already know so that I start at the right level instead of from absolute zero.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains: learningGoal (enum), proficiencyLevel (enum: beginner | elementary | intermediate), onboardingCompleted (boolean)
Onboarding flow is shown once when onboardingCompleted === false
PATCH /users/me/onboarding saves the collected fields and sets onboardingCompleted = true
Proficiency level gates which lessons appear first on the lesson map
Weekly goal set during onboarding pre-populates F-07 goal

features.name: Audio Pronunciation
features.babbel_analog: Native speaker audio on every word
features.description: Each word can have an associated audio clip (CDN URL) that plays automatically on word reveal during a quiz or flashcard session. Learners can also tap the word in any context to replay the audio. In V1, TTS synthesis is acceptable; native recordings are preferred for V2.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to hear Uzbek words pronounced correctly so that I build accurate pronunciation habits from the start.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema gains optional audioUrl (string) field
Audio plays automatically when a word is revealed in flashcard or after-answer states
A speaker icon is shown on any word display; tapping it replays the audio
Audio is gracefully skipped (no error shown) when audioUrl is null
Admin tooling or a seed script can bulk-update audioUrl values

features.name: Achievement Badges
features.description: Badges are awarded server-side when milestone conditions are met. A badge notification appears in the UI after the triggering action. Badges are displayed on the user's profile page.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to earn badges for meaningful milestones so that my progress is marked with discrete moments of celebration.
acceptance_criteria: User schema gains badges array: [{ id, awardedAt }]
Initial badge set: FirstReview (complete first review), TenStrong (10 words at Strong), SevenStreak (7-day streak), FiftyLearned (50 words isLearned), FirstLesson (complete first lesson)
Badge conditions are evaluated server-side after POST /srs/review
Newly awarded badges are returned in the POST /srs/review response
Frontend shows a badge unlock animation/toast when a new badge is returned
Profile page lists all earned badges with award dates

features.name: Matching Pairs Exercise
features.description: A grid of Uzbek words and their English translations shown in shuffled order. The learner taps a word and then taps its matching translation; matched pairs disappear. A session is complete when all pairs are matched. Good for studying a new lesson's vocabulary in bulk.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want a matching exercise so that I can associate a batch of new words with their translations in an active, game-like way.
acceptance_criteria: Matching exercise is available as a lesson introduction mode (not a review mode)
Grids contain 4–6 pairs; drawn from the current lesson's words
Correct matches are visually confirmed and removed from the grid
Incorrect matches briefly flash red and remain on the grid
Completion triggers SRS enrollment for matched words (sets nextReview = tomorrow)

out_of_scope.reason: Babbel discontinued this for consumer use in 2025; operationally complex and not viable for a small team
High infrastructure cost; mature Uzbek NLP models do not yet exist
Babbel intentionally omits these; adds complexity without proven retention benefit for serious learners
Requires significant Uzbek audio content production; addressable once core loop is stable
Premature given current word count; the lesson/topic structure in F-05 is sufficient for this stage
